steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Transitions question. The blank connects two sentences describing two different solar eclipses: an annular eclipse on July 9, 1926, and a partial eclipse on December 24, 1927. These are separate events on different dates with different visual effects, so the transition must signal contrast or a shift to a different case.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — As a result,: signals that the second event was caused by the first — the 1927 partial eclipse was not caused by the 1926 annular eclipse; they are unrelated events. ✗
  • Choice B — On the other hand,: signals contrast or a shift to a different case — the second eclipse is a different type (partial vs. annular) on a different date, making contrast the right relationship. ✓
  • Choice C — Simultaneously,: signals that both events happened at the same time — the eclipses occurred on different dates (July 1926 vs. December 1927), so they could not have been simultaneous. ✗
  • Choice D — Indeed,: signals that the second statement reinforces or confirms the first — a different type of eclipse on a different date does not reinforce the first; it presents a separate, contrasting example. ✗
B is correct.
